mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and pushing S&P 500 forward returns positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0 percent, or the signal's predictive IC drops below zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and industrial production indicate collapsing aggregate demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and forces S&P 500 forward returns negative over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of high-inflation episodes, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL momentum and SPX returns flips sign over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the total addressable market for corporate earnings, directly translating into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns across all market cycles.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 60-day SPX returns (p > 0.05), or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding contracts for two consecutive months, household deleveraging suppresses discretionary spending, causing corporate earnings revisions downward and driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances following consecutive RSAFS declines, or the signal's information ratio drops below zero across multiple market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with stable consumer prices indicates healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure, which supports corporate margin growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid employment recovery, rising payroll employment boosts household income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and pushes S&P 500 forward returns positive over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150000 in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 150000 threshold, or the signal's predictive power disappears after adjusting for FEDFUNDS changes.
